paper.title: New candidate hypervelocity red clump stars in the inner Galactic bulge

extraction.summary: 69 candidate hypervelocity stars extracted: 4 with Gaia DR3 counterparts and 65 VIRAC2-only. All have projected tangential velocities exceeding the local escape velocity and proper motions pointing away from the Galactic centre.

method_chain.summary: VIRAC2 preliminary near-infrared astrometry and photometry, and Gaia DR3 optical astrometry/photometry.
Cross-match VIRAC2 with Gaia DR3 using 0.5 arcsec tolerance; remove spurious matches via G-Ks colour outliers.
Select red clump stars via Ks luminosity function, CMD location within 0.3<(J-Ks)0<1 and -0.1<(H-Ks)0<0.4, and H-Ks vs J-Ks colour-colour diagram.
Remove bright foreground stars with parallax pi>0.2 mas and sigma_pi/pi<0.5; remove M-dwarf contaminants via colour cuts from West et al. 2011.
Heliocentric distance from RC absolute magnitude M_Ks=-1.61 with population correction for age/metallicity mix using Salaris & Girardi 2002 models.
Correct VIRAC2 proper motions for solar reflex motion using (U,V,W)=(12.9,245.6,7.78) km/s with the gala/Astropy packages.
Monte-Carlo sampling of 100,000 proper motion and distance draws to derive tangential velocity distribution; median taken as characteristic V_tan.
Adopt MWPotential2014+DehnenBarPotential+Kepler potential from galpy to compute local escape velocity as a function of galactocentric distance.
Compare lower-limit tangential velocity (16th percentile) to local escape velocity; compute exceedance probability P=V_tan,16th/V_esc(r_GC).
Require projected 2D velocity vector to approach within 0.5 deg of the GC after solar reflex correction; criterion based on Speedystar Hills-mechanism simulations.
Flight time reported by the paper based on backward projection of proper motion vectors toward the GC.

inclusion_assessment.summary: Paper explicitly presents this star as a candidate hypervelocity star with tangential velocity exceeding the local escape velocity and proper motion pointing away from the Galactic centre.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Paper explicitly identifies this star as a candidate HVS with tangential velocity exceeding the local escape velocity and proper motion pointing away from the GC.

source_refs.context: Four sources fulfil the HVS criterion: projected 2D velocity vector approaches GC within 0.5 deg
Table caption listing the four candidate HVSs with Gaia DR3 counterpart
Conclusions: 69 HVS candidates identified in the MW bulge, 4 with Gaia DR3 counterpart

candidate_groups_considered.description: Gaia DR3-VIRAC2 crossmatch stars with lower-limit tangential velocity exceeding the local escape velocity.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: 46 stars have V_tan>V_esc, but only 4 have proper motions pointing away from the GC within 0.5deg and are retained as HVS candidates. The remaining 42 do not satisfy the origin-direction criterion.

source_refs.context: 46 high-velocity stars after M-dwarf removal
Only 4 of the 46 fulfil the HVS criterion of pointing away from GC

candidate_groups_considered.description: VIRAC2-only stars (no Gaia DR3 counterpart) with lower-limit tangential velocity exceeding the local escape velocity.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: 472 stars have V_tan>V_esc after foreground and M-dwarf cleaning, but only 65 have proper motions pointing away from the GC within 0.5deg and are retained as HVS candidates.

source_refs.context: 472 final sources with tangential velocities up to 2468 km/s
Only 65 of the 472 appear ejected from within 0.5deg of GC

candidate_groups_considered.description: Stars identified as possible M-dwarf contaminants via colour cuts from West et al. 2011.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: These objects are foreground contaminants and are not presented as possibly unbound from the Galaxy.

source_refs.context: Three sources fall into M-dwarf selection cuts in Gaia crossmatch
Nine possible M-dwarfs identified in VIRAC2-only sample via colour cuts
